hero.thesis: Podcasters need consistent, engaging social media content, but lack the time. You provide a service to cut 30 engaging clips per episode, offering value and saving them time.
hero.marketSize: The podcasting industry is projected to reach $4 billion by 2024. With over 850,000 active podcasts, demand for social media clips is skyrocketing.
hero.payingEvidence: Podcasters are already spending $300-$700 per episode on editing and promotional services.

ninetyDay.title: Setup Foundations

actions.action: Register your business using Stripe Atlas for LLC formation.
Set up payment processing through Stripe to handle invoicing and payments.
Create a portfolio showcasing sample clips using Framer.

ninetyDay.metric: Business setup completed and portfolio live.

ninetyDay.title: Identify and Engage

actions.action: Join and engage in the PodcastPros Discord server, introduce your service.
Compile a list of 50 target podcasts using ListenUp directory.
Craft personalized cold emails for outreach using Apollo.

ninetyDay.metric: Contact 10 podcasts, receive at least 2 responses.

ninetyDay.title: First Clients and Feedback

actions.action: Send cold emails to 10 targeted podcasts from your list.
Follow up with podcasts that haven’t responded yet.
Secure your first client by offering a trial discount for the first episode.

ninetyDay.metric: Sign the first client, complete 1 trial episode.

ninetyDay.title: Streamline Your Process

actions.action: Develop a Notion board to track client projects and deadlines.
actions.success: Notion board set up for project management.

actions.action: Create clip templates in Lovable to ensure consistency and speed.
Automate repeated tasks with n8n for efficiency.
actions.success: Automations in place for repetitive tasks.

ninetyDay.metric: Reduce editing time by 20% per episode.

actions.action: Launch a beehiiv newsletter targeting podcasters with tips and insights.
Create a LinkedIn page for your service and connect with 50 podcasters.
Post weekly updates and success stories on LinkedIn.
Gather feedback from current clients about your service.
Adjust your service packages based on feedback.
Update your portfolio and marketing materials to reflect changes.
Attend a virtual podcasting conference, like PodSummit.
Join a Facebook group for podcast producers.
actions.success: Joined and engaged in one group.

actions.action: Reach out to 5 new contacts made during the week.

ninetyDay.title: Optimize Workflows

actions.action: Use Make to create advanced workflows for clip production.
Document your processes for future team members.
Test new workflows with a client project.

ninetyDay.metric: Increase output to 35 clips per episode.

actions.action: Develop a content plan for your beehiiv newsletter.
actions.success: Content plan created for next 3 months.

actions.action: Write a guest blog post for a podcasting website.
Share your guest blog post on social channels.
actions.success: Blog post shared on all channels.

actions.action: Introduce a loyalty program for long-term clients.
Schedule monthly check-ins with clients via Calendly.
Send personalized thank-you notes to clients.
actions.success: Thank-you notes sent to all clients.

actions.action: Expand your email list using Apollo to include 100 more leads.
Launch a referral program for current clients.
Reach out to podcast directories to feature your service.
Review financials and assess profitability.
Plan next quarter's goals and strategies.
actions.success: Goals and strategies planned for next quarter.

actions.action: Celebrate reaching $3,800 MRR milestone with team or solo.

toolStack.name: Framer
toolStack.why: For creating an impressive portfolio of your work.

toolStack.name: Stripe Atlas
toolStack.why: To quickly and easily set up an LLC.

toolStack.name: Apollo
toolStack.why: To streamline cold outreach and build your contact list.

toolStack.name: Lovable
toolStack.why: To design and maintain consistent clip templates.

toolStack.name: n8n
toolStack.why: To automate repetitive tasks, reducing manual work.

toolStack.name: Notion
toolStack.why: For project management and process documentation.

firstTenCustomers.script:
Subject: [Name], Enhance Your Podcast's Reach with Engaging Clips!

Hi [Name],

I help podcasters like you save time by creating 30 engaging clips from each episode that maximize social media impact, driving more listeners to your podcast. Let's transform your episodes into captivating content that resonates with your audience.

I’d love to offer you a trial discount for your first episode to demonstrate the impact engaging clips can have.

Looking forward to hearing from you,

[Your Name]
firstTenCustomers.cadence: Initial email, follow-up after 3 days, second follow-up after 1 week.

pricing.raisingRules: Raise prices by 10% after every 10 clients, ensuring demand stays strong.

failureModes: Underestimating time per episode - leads to burnout.
Ignoring niche focus - losing unique selling proposition.
Poor quality clips - results in client dissatisfaction.
Lack of process documentation - halts scale.
Inconsistent marketing - leads to fluctuating client acquisition.

setup.llc: Use Stripe Atlas to form your LLC quickly and efficiently.
setup.payments: Set up Stripe for handling all client payments and invoicing.
setup.contracts: Utilize standard service agreement contracts from LegalZoom to protect both parties.
setup.insurance: Consider getting professional liability insurance through Hiscox.

scaling: Once you hit $5,000 in MRR, consider hiring a part-time assistant to help with editing and client management. Use the assistant to handle repetitive tasks, freeing you to focus on client acquisition and quality control. As demand increases, bring on additional editors on a per-project basis to maintain flexibility. Ensure all processes are documented in Notion to streamline onboarding and maintain service consistency. As you grow, consider shifting to a subscription model for steady cash flow and easier scaling.
